articles.title: HMD Thunder Pro specs and images leak
articles.description: A new HMD smartphone is on the way, according to a reliable account on X that has leaked HMD-related information in the past. It's going to be called HMD Thunder Pro when it lands. It has a 6.67-inch OLED screen with FHD+ resolution and 90Hz refresh rate, a 50MP main camera with OIS, an 8MP ultrawide, a 50MP front camera, and a 6,000 mAh battery with support for 20W wired charging. It's powered by the Unsioc T620 SoC, paired with 8GB of RAM and 256GB of UFS 2.2 storage. The phone supports NFC and has a 3.5mm headphone jack, two mics, two speakers, and an IP65 rating for dust and...

articles.description: Symbiosis Institute of Technology has released the SITEEE 2026 results on set-test.org. Candidates can download their scorecards using their SITEEE ID and password. The exam, held in two phases, assessed candidates in Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics. Qualified students will proceed to the counselling process, with admissions also possible via JEE Main or MHT CET scores.

Police arrested three individuals in connection with the murder, with the main accused, a married man from Canada, reportedly pressuring the victim for marriage. The murder weapon and vehicle were recovered from the canal.

articles.title: eSIM by AT&T launches for World Cup visitors with unlimited data
articles.description: AT&T has just announced the launch of a new service called eSIM by AT&T. It's aimed at people visiting the US, Canada, and/or Mexico, and the choice of countries makes sense when you consider the fact that the FIFA World Cup is taking place from June 11 to July 19 in exactly those three countries. AT&T is aiming this at people who will be arriving in North America for the World Cup matches. As you can see below, eSIM by AT&T comes in four 'sizes'. A one-day pass is $3.99 and only available for the US. A seven-day pass is $15.99 for just the US, or $24.99 for all three countries. A...

articles.title: Hindustan Aeronautics Limited bets big with HR reset
articles.description: Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL), India’s premier defence PSU, has long been seen as hierarchical and resistant to change. On May 6, 2026, just days after the new Chairman and Managing Director assumed office, it announced dramatic HR reforms, including the formation of a Central HR Cell (CHRC) in Bengaluru, effective June 1.
